notes: Legacy v1 regression set (10 cases). Migrated to evals/exhaustive-suite.json (v2, 35+ cases with visual-quality, html-contract, and explore-mode categories). Retained for routing.md and benchmark compatibility.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to explore layout changes to the question tool described in evals/files/question-tool.md.
evals.expected_output: A standalone artifact editor playground with live preview, meaningful presets, stateful controls, inspector, export prompt, and source assumptions.
assertions: Chooses artifact editor as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes live preview, presets, direct controls, reset, and export/paste-back output.
Uses the source fixture and labels source assumptions instead of inventing the question tool.
Uses topic-specific visual language tied to question layout, answer choices, confidence, and decision state.
Does not present three unrelated visual concepts unless the user asks for options.
Does not route to production frontend implementation.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to show how the email agent in evals/files/email-agent-source.md works and let me comment on nodes.
evals.expected_output: A canvas/node map playground with source-grounded modules, click-to-comment, filters, node inspector, source notes, and export feedback.
assertions: Chooses canvas/node map as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Uses the provided source fixture instead of inventing a codebase.
Includes click-to-comment or select-node-to-comment behavior.
Includes node filters, inspector/details panel, source assumptions, and export feedback.
Avoids generic dashboard styling and static-only diagrams.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to review evals/files/sample-skill.md and give inline suggestions I can approve, reject, or comment on. Do not create files.
evals.expected_output: A read-only review surface response with anchored suggestions, approve/reject/comment states, review export shape, and no artifact file creation.
assertions: Does not create files because the prompt says not to.
Chooses review surface as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes anchored suggestions tied to the sample skill.
Uses the sample skill source and names source assumptions.
Includes approve, reject, and comment states.
Defines export output containing only approved suggestions, with rejected items labeled separately.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to help me balance the Inferno hero deck in evals/files/inferno-hero-deck.json.
evals.expected_output: A simulator playground with coupled variables, derived metrics, warnings, balance assumptions, and recommended patch output.
assertions: Chooses simulator as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes coupled variables, scenario switches or presets, derived metrics, and warnings.
Distinguishes real fixture stats from inferred balance assumptions.
Exports a recommended patch prompt.
Does not treat deck as a presentation unless the source indicates a slide deck.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to turn the design review narrative in evals/files/design-review-narrative.md into interactive HTML.
evals.expected_output: Routes through design:present first, then proposes a narrative walkthrough playground with objection drawer, decision checkpoints, appendix, and exportable meeting script.
assertions: Routes through design:present before building the walkthrough.
Chooses narrative walkthrough as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Uses the source narrative and labels source assumptions.
Includes section rail, objection drawer, decision checkpoints, appendix, and export output.
Does not clone a static deck.
Does not route to harden or production frontend implementation.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to make the copy in evals/files/copy-states.md sharper and show it as an interactive state explorer.
evals.expected_output: Routes through design:content-design first, then proposes a copy/state explorer with state variants, risk/tone notes, comments, and approved-copy export.
assertions: Routes through design:content-design before the playground.
Chooses review surface or lens explorer as the interaction model for copy/state exploration and explains why.
Uses the copy-state source fixture and labels source assumptions.
Includes user state, system state, risk, tone, CTA, and state variants.
Includes approve/reject/comment or selected-copy export.
Does not treat copy as decorative labels.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to generate visual inspiration first for an architecture playground based on evals/files/email-agent-source.md.
evals.expected_output: Routes through imagegen for one inspiration seed, extracts visual grammar, and rebuilds the semantics in HTML/SVG/canvas instead of using wallpaper.
assertions: Routes through imagegen because the user explicitly asked for visual inspiration first.
Chooses a source-grounded architecture interaction model after image seeding.
Uses one inspiration seed rather than multiple visual directions.
Extracts composition, depth, texture, lighting, rhythm, density, material, or spatial metaphor.
Keeps essential controls, labels, state, and export outside the bitmap.
Does not use generated image as wallpaper.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to create a static diagram of the API flow in evals/files/api-flow.md.
evals.expected_output: A static API-flow artifact is allowed only with explicit justification that interaction adds no understanding, plus source notes, legend, assumptions, and export summary.
assertions: States why static output is sufficient for this API-flow request.
Considers interaction and explains why it would not add understanding for the static diagram request.
Uses the API-flow source instead of inventing endpoints.
Includes actors, services, request/response order, branches, and trust boundaries.
Includes source notes, assumptions, legend, and export/paste-back output.
Does not omit the artifact contract just because output is static.

evals.prompt: Show three different visual concepts for the onboarding dashboard.
evals.expected_output: Routes to design:diverge instead of playground.
assertions: Does not use playground as the primary skill.
Routes to design:diverge because the user asked for multiple concepts.
Does not build an artifact before direction selection.

evals.prompt: Build this as production UI in the app route.
evals.expected_output: Routes to frontend-design or the app-specific implementation skill instead of playground.
assertions: Does not use playground as the primary skill.
Routes to frontend-design or an app-specific development skill.
Does not create a standalone playground as the final production implementation.
